conversation.statement: Good timing. I was just about to start processing the scene.

The victim is Dr. Justine Phillips, one of the doctors that work here. She was found by her assistant, Anna Steward.
It looks like a suicide, but we need to make sure it really is.

No, but I contacted the security firm that controls after hour access to the building.
They'll email the security access log to headquarters.
Yes. It's crucial to document the body's position after a shooting.

First, take pictures in the victim's office. Then make sure you take samples of the blood stains on the wall and on the floor with your swabs.
You should also talk to the assistant.
You brought one from headquarters, right? It should be in your tools.

Use the swabs Russell gave you.
And don't forget to search the scene for any other evidence.
conversation.response: Where do I find a camera?
How do I take blood stain samples?

conversation.name: HospitalNotDone
conversation.statement: How can I help you?
No. Before we leave, you should
pick up the victim's computer.
photograph the scene.
use your swabs to take samples of the bloodstains on the wall and on the floor.
pick up the gun the victim is holding in her hand.

conversation.name: HospitalDone
conversation.statement: How can I help you?
Looks like you have gathered all the evidence there is.

conversation.name: Visit2
conversation.statement:
So, the victim was somehow involved with the death of this Terry McCoy's daughter?
Let's see if we can find anything about this case.
Maybe there's a file in the victim's office.
The victim's colleague, Dr. Brody Fowler, has arrived. Make sure you talk to him.
conversation.response: What else should I do here?

conversation.name: Visit2NotDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
I think you still need to
search for more evidence in the victim's office.
ask Dr. Fowler about the subpoena.

conversation.name: Visit2Done
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
Yes. Let's get out of here.

conversation.name: McCoy1
conversation.statement: The threatening emails McCoy sent our victim make him our number one person of interest.
We have a warrant to search the place, but make sure you talk to him first. Maybe he's cooperative.

We need a DNA sample to find out if the skin tissue you found under the victim's fingernails is from him.
Also, search the place for a gun.

conversation.name: McCoyNotDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
I think you still need to
talk to McCoy.
find more evidence.
use your swabs to collect a DNA sample from McCoy.

conversation.name: McCoyDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
Yes. Let's get out of here.

conversation.name: Pharmacy1
conversation.statement: Let's see if Edgar White has some information for us.

conversation.name: PharmacyNotDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
I think you still need to talk to White.

conversation.name: PharmacyDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
Yes. Let's move on.

conversation.name: Pharmacy2
conversation.statement: I wonder what the payment to the pharmacist was for. Five thousand bucks seems like a lot.
Ask White about the money, and let him know that we have a warrant to search the pharmacy.
To determine if the skin tissue you found under the victim's fingernails is from him, we'll need a DNA sample. Also, if he has a gun, take it.

conversation.name: PharmacyNotDone2
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
I think you still need to
talk to White.
find more evidence.
use your swabs to collect a DNA sample from White.

conversation.name: PharmacyDone2
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
Yes. Let's go back to the headquarters and analyze the evidence we have.

conversation.name: PharmacyIgnore
conversation.statement: Let's not waste our time here.

conversation.name: Assistant1
conversation.statement: Anna Steward was at the crime scene around the time our victim died. I'm curious what she has to say about that.
Since we don't have a warrant, you need to ask her for permission to search her home. And don't forget to ask about her late night visit to the crime scene.
You still haven't found a match for the skin tissue underneath the victim's fingernails. Ask her if you can get a DNA sample. Also, search the place for a weapon.
conversation.response: Is there any evidence I can collect here?

conversation.name: AssistantNotDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
I think you still need to
talk to Steward.
use your swabs to collect a DNA sample from Steward.

conversation.name: AssistantDone
conversation.statement: Do you have a question?
Yes. Time to go back to headquarters.
